prompt: Draw a clean academic systems diagram on warm white. HARD RULE: exactly one box for each component label; never duplicate Built-in Tools, MCP Servers, Plugins, Hooks, Skills, or any other label. Make a compact SOURCE CLUSTER at far left: Plugins is the leftmost box; immediately to its right stack four unique boxes vertically: Built-in Tools, MCP Servers, Hooks, Skills. Plugins has thin arrows to MCP Servers, Hooks, and Skills. Built-in Tools and MCP Servers feed Tool Pool. Across the upper half draw one straight main pipeline: Tool Pool -> Visible Schemas -> Model Tool Call -> Tool Router -> Permission Gate -> Execution -> Tool Result. Hooks has exactly one outgoing teal arrow directly to Tool Router, labeled before / after. Skills has exactly one outgoing green arrow directly to Model Context at lower right, bypassing the entire main pipeline. Tool Result has one red arrow down to Model Context. CRITICAL INVARIANTS: one box per label; Skills -> Model Context; Hooks -> Tool Router; never Hooks -> Model Context; never Skills -> Tool Router. Use thick unambiguous arrows, generous whitespace, no crossing lines, restrained flat teal/blue/green/amber/coral/gray colors. Visible labels only: Built-in Tools, MCP Servers, Plugins, Hooks, Skills, Tool Pool, Visible Schemas, Model Tool Call, Tool Router, Permission Gate, Execution, Tool Result, Model Context, before / after. No gradients, no 3D, no watermark, no invented components.
